2026-04-13.content:
You can use [Filestore CSI driver](https://docs.cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/persistent-volumes/filestore-csi-driver) to dynamically configure custom performance for zonal and regional instances using the Kubernetes `VolumeAttributesClass`.

For more information, see [Dynamically scale Filestore performance using VolumeAttributesClass](filestore/docs/csi-driver#custom-performance).

summary.title: Filestore CSI driver performance scaling
summary.summary: You can now dynamically configure custom performance for Filestore instances using the Kubernetes VolumeAttributesClass. This feature allows for greater flexibility in managing storage performance for your workloads. Learn more about how to leverage this new capability for your applications.

2026-04-09.content:
Filestore is integrated with [Backup and Disaster Recovery (DR) Service](https://docs.cloud.google.com/backup-disaster-recovery/docs) allowing you to centrally manage your backups with advanced features for data protection.
This feature is [generally available](https://cloud.google.com/products?#product-launch-stages) for Filestore instances.

For more information, see [Backups overview](https://docs.cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/backups).

summary.title: Filestore backup integration
summary.summary: Filestore now integrates with the Backup and Disaster Recovery service, enabling centralized backup management and advanced data protection features. This new capability is generally available for Filestore instances.

2026-01-20.content: Filestore is available in `asia-southeast3` (Bangkok). For more information, see [Filestore regions and zones](https://docs.cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/regions).

summary.title: Filestore now in Bangkok
summary.summary: You can now use Filestore in the asia-southeast3 region (Bangkok). This expansion provides more options for data storage and access in Southeast Asia. For more details on supported regions, please refer to the Filestore documentation.

2025-10-20.content:
You can now create regional Filestore instances starting at 100 GiB and scale them in 1 GiB units. The small instances feature is available under Restricted GA in `us-central1` and `us-east4` regions.
For more information, see [Small capacity Filestore instances](https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/service-tiers#small-instances).

summary.title: Filestore small instances
summary.summary: You can now create regional Filestore instances starting at 100 GiB and scale them in 1 GiB units.

2025-06-04.content:
The [promoteReplica API](https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/reference/rest/v1/projects.locations.instances/promoteReplica) is available for promoting replicas to regular instances.
For more information, see [About instance replication](https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/instance-replication).

summary.title: New API available for replica promotion
summary.summary: The promoteReplica API is now available to promote replicas to regular instances. This feature enhances instance management by allowing replicas to be promoted.

2025-05-27.content: You can now use the [Filestore CSI driver](https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/persistent-volumes/filestore-csi-driver) to create Filestore instances with the NFSv4.1 protocol from the Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) cluster. This feature is available for zonal, regional, and enterprise [service tiers](https://cloud.google.com/filestore/docs/service-tiers).

summary.title: Filestore CSI driver available on GKE
summary.summary: You can now create Filestore instances using the Filestore CSI driver on GKE. This supports NFSv4.1 protocol and is available across various service tiers, including zonal, regional, and enterprise.
